attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who excels in chaotic finale scenarios rather than pure power attacks. Wins by superior positioning and tactical awareness when stronger riders falter in disorganized sprints.
team_tactics: Insufficient data - appears to operate with limited team support, relying on individual tactical acumen in breakaways rather than coordinated lead-outs.
terrain_preference: Thrives in technical, chaotic finales where positioning and bike-handling trump pure power. Breakaway stages where organizational breakdown creates opportunities for tactically astute riders.
vulnerability: Likely struggles when races remain controlled and organized, requiring pure physical dominance rather than tactical opportunism. May lack firepower against dedicated sprinters or climbers in controlled scenarios.
key_patterns: Succeeds from doomed breakaways through superior final kilometer chaos management (Giro 2018 win)
Overperforms expectations as underdog without strong team support (contract-less 2017, breakaway win 2018)
Capitalizes on positioning errors by stronger riders when race organization breaks down
form_trajectory: positive: Stage win at 2018 Giro demonstrated ability to execute at WorldTour level after rebuilding from contract-less 2017 season
